The analogist is decisive: **Route R (rigidity / Milne §I.3) is materially less blocked** — its keystone (valuative criterion) is in Mathlib, while Route H needs three from-scratch foundational builds (Ω sheaf, coherent-sheaf cohomology + Serre H⁰ = "the single largest gap", scheme Frobenius). Combined with my Milne reads (cube absent from both genus-0 AND Albanese-UP paths), the decision is clear. Let me update STRATEGY.md.

input.old_string:
### Genus-0 rigidity — base-case route under re-examination

The genus-0 witness OBJECT is the trivial `J = Spec k` (skeleton 6/7 closed); it
needs **no** Picard scheme. Remaining content: over `k̄`, a genus-0 curve is
`≅ ℙ¹` and every pointed `f : C → A` killing `P` is constant. The **Rigidity Lemma**
(Mumford Form I, §4) is being formalized now as a foundational, route-independent
asset — it yields Milne Cor 1.5 (additivity of `Hom(−,A)` over products), which
Route A's Albanese UP needs regardless, so it is not wasted under any base-case
choice. The base case `ℙ¹→A constant` has three candidate routes; the doubled
budget re-opens the ranking:

- **(c-cube) char-free via the theorem of the cube.** Milne Prop 3.10: the
  multi-factor induction is cube-free (Cor 1.5), but the single-curve base case
  `ℙ¹→A constant` rests on the theorem of the cube (Mumford §6/§10). The cube drags
  seesaw + flat/proper cohomology base-change + semicontinuity + line bundles on
  products — ALL zero-Mathlib, ~8–15+ iters, "comparable to a chunk of
  representability." **Justified ONLY IF** the cube is genuinely shared with
  Route A's Albanese UP (Milne §III.6) — currently **UNVERIFIED**; this is the
  gating check before any cube investment. If not shared, (c-cube) pays the full
  cube cost as a pure genus-0 tax.
- **(c-hybrid) cube-free, differential + Frobenius descent.** Via `C≅ℙ¹` (the RR
  bridge any route needs): `Ω_A ≅ O_A^g` (the `cotangentSpaceAtIdentity` template,
  ~800–1500 LOC, already scoped); `df : f^*Ω_A → Ω_{ℙ¹}=O(−2)` is zero because
  `H⁰(ℙ¹,O(−2))=0` is **elementary on the concrete `ℙ¹`** (the general-curve
  Serre-duality refutation does NOT touch `ℙ¹`); char-`0`: `df=0 ⟹ const`; char-`p`:
  `f` factors through relative Frobenius `ℙ¹→ℙ¹⁽ᵖ⁾≅ℙ¹`, and a degree argument forces
  `const`. Avoids the cube entirely, reuses already-built chart-algebra + GrpObj
  assets. The relative-Frobenius-factorization piece is not yet cost-estimated.
- **(b) byproduct of Route A.** Once `Pic⁰` exists, `dim Pic⁰ = genus = 0 ⟹ Pic⁰`
  trivial `⟹ Alb(C)=0`; needs neither cube nor RR bridge, but couples genus-0 to
  Route A's `Pic⁰` + dimension theory (the ~40–70-iter monster). Note (c)'s
  decoupling only insures the *post-cube* A.2 risk, since (c-cube) also needs the
  cube.

Decisive constraint: char-freeness (protected goal over arbitrary `[Field k]`)
rules out the **naive** `df=0`-only route, but NOT (c-hybrid) (Frobenius handles
char-`p`) and NOT (c-cube). The base-case re-decision (the next genus-0 deliverable)
turns on two facts to establish first: (1) is the cube actually required AND built
for Route A's Albanese UP? (2) what is the Frobenius-factorization cost in (c-hybrid)?

### (a) fallback — differential via Serre duality (off path)

`df=0` for a general genus-0 curve via {`Ω_A` cotangent-triviality + Serre duality
`H^0(C,Ω_C)=0`}, reusing the closed axiom-clean chart-algebra envelope (KDM,
`constants_integral_over_base_field`, `df_zero_factors_through_constant_on_chart`,
the converse `df=0 ⟹ const`). Kept in tree, off path; carries a char-`p` gap. Note
(c-hybrid) supersedes most of this by going through the concrete `ℙ¹` (no Serre
duality) — if a differential route is taken, (c-hybrid) is the live form.

The genus-0 witness OBJECT is the trivial `J = Spec k` (skeleton 6/7 closed); it
needs **no** Picard scheme. Remaining content: over `k̄`, every pointed `f : C → A`
from a genus-0 curve to an abelian variety is constant. The **Rigidity Lemma**
(Mumford Form I = Milne Rigidity Thm 1.1) is **PROVEN axiom-clean (iter-162)** and
is the foundation of this route. Milne §I.3 derives `ℙ¹→A constant` from it with
**NO theorem of the cube** (verified by reading the source, iter-163). Chain:

- **Cor 1.5** (additivity): `h : V×W → A`, `h(v₀,w₀)=0 ⟹ h = f∘p + g∘q` uniquely.
  Direct from the proven Rigidity Lemma (the difference `h − (f∘p+g∘q)` collapses on
  both axes). **No-regret** — Route A's Albanese UP (Milne Prop 6.4) also needs it.
- **Cor 1.2** (regular map of AVs sending `0↦0` is a homomorphism). From Cor 1.5.
- **Thm 3.2** (rational map from a nonsingular variety to an AV is everywhere
  defined). Via the valuative criterion of properness (in Mathlib:
  `IsProper.of_valuativeCriterion`, `ValuativeCriterion`) + indeterminacy-locus pure
  codim 1 (Milne Lemma 3.3). The surface case (`ℙ¹×ℙ¹`) is the riskiest piece.
- **`Hom(𝔾_a,A)=0`** + the `𝔾_a/𝔾_m` incompatibility (Milne Prop 3.9): a homomorphism
  from the additive group to an AV is trivial. Char-free.
- **genus-0⟹ℙ¹** (RR bridge, Hartshorne IV.1.3.5) — shared, no Mathlib RR.

Assembly: Prop 3.9 (`ℙ¹→A const`) = Thm 3.2 (extend the difference map) + Cor 1.2 +
`𝔾_a/𝔾_m`; the headline `rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` = Prop 3.9 + the iso.
**Route C is uniformly char-free** (no differentials, no Frobenius). Mathlib-support
survey (`analogies/route-support.md`): Route C far less blocked than the differential
route (valuative criterion present; the differential route needs Ω-sheaf + coherent
cohomology + Serre `H⁰` + scheme Frobenius, all from scratch).

### Off-path fallbacks (kept in tree, NOT pursued)

- **(c-hybrid) differential + Frobenius.** `Ω_A≅O^g` + `df=0` on the concrete `ℙ¹`
  (char 0) + relative Frobenius factorization (char `p`). Reuses chart-algebra +
  GrpObj. REJECTED iter-163: three from-scratch Mathlib foundations (Ω-sheaf, ℙ¹
  line-bundle cohomology, scheme Frobenius) vs Route C's single contained surface gap.
- **(c-cube) theorem of the cube.** EXCISED iter-163: reading Milne §I.3 + §III.6
  showed the cube is on NEITHER the genus-0 base case NOR Route A's Albanese UP — it
  would be a pure ~8–15-iter zero-Mathlib tax.
- **(b) byproduct of Route A** (`dim Pic⁰=0`). Couples genus-0 to the ~40–70-iter
  Route A monster; not pursued as an independent near-term route.
- **(a) Serre-duality `df=0`** for a general genus-0 curve, reusing the closed
  chart-algebra envelope (KDM converse `df=0 ⟹ const`). Char-`p` gap; superseded by
  Route C. `rigidity_over_kbar` (`[CharZero]`) in `RigidityKbar.lean` is its artifact.

  already PROVED axiom-clean (iter-162); its proof uses only completeness + "map from a complete

# Blueprint-writer directive (iter-163) — rewrite the base-case section of AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex for Route C (Milne §I.3); CORRECT the cube error

## Chapter to edit (ONLY this file)
`blueprint/src/chapters/AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex`

## Strategic context (the slice that matters)
The Rigidity-Lemma chain (`thm:rigidity_lemma` and its sub-lemmas) is PROVEN axiom-clean and must
NOT be touched. What changes is the **base-case section** — "morphisms from ℙ¹ to an abelian
variety are constant" and the genus-0 headline. The project has DECIDED (iter-163) to complete the
genus-0 arm via **Route C = Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, §I.3** (NOT the theorem of the cube, NOT
the differential/Frobenius route).

## CRITICAL CORRECTION (the chapter currently contains a mathematical error)
The current chapter asserts — in `\section{The theorem of the cube (deferred deep input)}`, in the
`\uses{...thm:theorem_of_the_cube}` edges of `prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant`, and in
`rmk:cube_is_load_bearing` — that the single-curve base case "ℙ¹→A constant" RESTS ON the theorem
of the cube. **This is FALSE.** Reading Milne §I.3 directly (verified iter-163):
Milne's Rigidity Theorem 1.1 IS the project's proven Rigidity Lemma; from it Milne derives
"ℙ¹→A constant" (Prop 3.9, the base case of Prop 3.10) WITHOUT the cube, using:
  - **Theorem 3.2**: a rational map from a nonsingular variety into an abelian variety is defined
    on the whole variety (proved via Theorem 3.1 = the valuative criterion of properness + Lemma
    3.3 = the indeterminacy locus of a rational map to a group variety is pure codimension 1);
  - **Corollary 1.2**: a regular map of abelian varieties carrying 0 to 0 is a homomorphism (from
    the Rigidity Theorem via the difference map);
  - **Corollary 1.5**: additivity — `h : V×W → A` with `h(v₀,w₀)=0` decomposes uniquely as
    `h = f∘p + g∘q` (a corollary of the Rigidity Theorem);
  - the **𝔾_a/𝔾_m incompatibility** (Prop 3.9): after translation `f|_{𝔸¹}` is an additive-group
    homomorphism `𝔾_a → A`; but `𝔸¹∖0 = 𝔾_m` is also a group variety giving `f(xy)=f(x)+f(y)+c`;
    the two structures are incompatible unless `f` is constant.
The theorem of the cube (Milne §I.5) is used ONLY for projectivity, the seesaw/square, dual abelian
varieties, polarizations, and the Poincaré sheaf — NONE on the genus-0 path. (For confirmation:
the Albanese universal property the positive-genus Route A needs, Milne Prop 6.1/6.4, is ALSO
cube-free — same Thm 3.2 + Cor 1.2 + Cor 1.5 ingredients.)

## Required structural changes

### (1) DELETE the cube section and the load-bearing remark
- Remove the entire `\section{The theorem of the cube (deferred deep input)}` block including
  `\begin{theorem}[Theorem of the cube]\label{thm:theorem_of_the_cube}...\end{theorem}` and its
  surrounding "Deferred prerequisite" prose.
- Remove `\begin{remark}\label{rmk:cube_is_load_bearing}...\end{remark}`.
- Remove every `\uses{...thm:theorem_of_the_cube...}` edge (in `prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant`'s
  statement AND proof). If you wish to record WHY the cube is not needed, write a SHORT new remark
  `rmk:cube_not_needed` stating that Milne §I.3/§III.6 derive both the genus-0 base case and the
  Albanese UP from the Rigidity Theorem + Thm 3.2 + Cor 1.2/1.5 with no theorem of the cube
  (cite Milne §I.3, §III.6) — keep it to ~4 lines, no verbatim cube quote needed.

### (2) ADD the Milne §I.3 decomposition (new lemma blocks, textbook-level proofs)
Add a new section, e.g. `\section{The Milne §I.3 chain: additivity, homomorphisms, extension}`,
BEFORE the "Morphisms from ℙ¹..." section, with these blocks (give each a `\label`, a `\lean{...}`
hint with the PROPOSED Lean name in the `AlgebraicGeometry` namespace, `\uses` edges, a textbook
proof, and full citation discipline — see below):

- `lem:hom_additivity_over_product` — **Milne Corollary 1.5.** Statement: let `V, W` be complete
  varieties over `k̄` with `k̄`-points `v₀, w₀`, `p, q` the projections `V×W→V`, `V×W→W`, and `A`
  an abelian variety; a morphism `h : V×W → A` with `h(v₀,w₀)=0` can be written uniquely as
  `h = f∘p + g∘q` with `f : V→A`, `g : W→A`, `f(v₀)=0`, `g(w₀)=0`. Proof: set
  `f = h|_{V×{w₀}}`, `g = h|_{{v₀}×W}`; the difference `φ = h − (f∘p + g∘q) : V×W → A` vanishes on
  both axes `V×{w₀}` and `{v₀}×W` (compute), so by the **Rigidity Lemma** (`thm:rigidity_lemma`,
  with `V` complete and `V×W` geometrically irreducible) `φ ≡ 0`; uniqueness because `f, g` are
  recovered as the axis-restrictions. `\uses{thm:rigidity_lemma}`. Proposed Lean name:
  `AlgebraicGeometry.hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` (the prover may rename).
- `lem:av_regular_map_is_hom` — **Milne Corollary 1.2.** A regular map `α : A → B` of abelian
  varieties is the composite of a homomorphism with a translation; equivalently if `α(0)=0` then
  `α` is a homomorphism. Proof: form `φ(a,a') = α(a+a') − α(a) − α(a') : A×A → B`; `φ` vanishes on
  `A×0` and `0×A`; Cor 1.5 / Rigidity forces `φ=0`. `\uses{lem:hom_additivity_over_product}`.
- `lem:rational_map_to_av_extends` — **Milne Theorem 3.2 (+ Thm 3.1, Lemma 3.3).** A rational map
  from a nonsingular variety to an abelian variety is defined on the whole variety. Proof sketch:
  Theorem 3.1 (valuative criterion of properness, Mathlib `IsProper.of_valuativeCriterion` /
  `ValuativeCriterion`) gives a representative defined outside codimension ≥ 2; Lemma 3.3 (the
  indeterminacy locus of a rational map to a GROUP variety is pure codimension 1, via the
  difference map `Φ(x,y)=φ(x)φ(y)⁻¹`) then shows there is no codimension-1 indeterminacy, so the
  map is everywhere defined. Flag clearly (in a `\begin{remark}`) that the codim-1 indeterminacy
  step (Lemma 3.3) has NO Mathlib Weil-divisor support and is the route's riskiest sub-build; note
  the open question whether a pointwise valuative extension at each codimension-1 point side-steps
  building divisor theory. `\uses{}` as appropriate. Proposed Lean name to be decided by the prover.
- `lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial` — **the 𝔾_a/𝔾_m core of Milne Prop 3.9.** A morphism `f : ℙ¹ → A`
  with `f(∞)=0`, restricted to `𝔾_a = 𝔸¹ = ℙ¹∖{∞}`, is (by Thm 3.2 + Cor 1.2) an additive-group
  homomorphism `f(x+y)=f(x)+f(y)`; restricting instead to `𝔾_m = 𝔸¹∖{0}` gives
  `f(xy)=f(x)+f(y)+c`; these are incompatible unless `f` is constant. Conclude `f` constant.
  `\uses{lem:av_regular_map_is_hom, lem:rational_map_to_av_extends}`.

### (3) REWRITE `prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant` proof as the Milne Prop 3.9 assembly
Replace the current cube-based proof body. New proof: `f : ℙ¹ → A` (already a morphism, ℙ¹ is
complete nonsingular so no extension needed for `f` itself); translate so `f(∞)=0`; apply
`lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial` to conclude `f` constant; un-translate. For the multi-factor Prop 3.10
(general unirational `V`) note the induction from Cor 1.5 reducing to one ℙ¹ factor, but only the
`d=1` base case is needed here. `\uses{lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial}` (and drop the cube edge). Keep the
existing verbatim Milne Prop 3.10 SOURCE QUOTE that is already in the block.

### (4) The headline `thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV` and `prop:genusZero_curve_iso_P1`
Leave `prop:genusZero_curve_iso_P1` (genus-0 ⟹ ℙ¹, Hartshorne IV.1.3.5) as-is — it is correct and
still needed. The headline proof is essentially correct (iso + ℙ¹-constancy); just ensure its
prose no longer references the cube and its `\uses` edges are consistent with the new chain.

## Citation discipline (MANDATORY — read the source yourself)
`pdftotext`/`pdftoppm` are NOT installed. Extract verbatim Milne text with a python snippet:

python3 -c "from pypdf import PdfReader; r=PdfReader('references/abelian-varieties.pdf'); print(r.pages[N].extract_text())"

10	
11	### Route: A — Picard/Quot/Hilbert FGA engine (positive-genus object)
12	
13	- **Goal-alignment**: PASS — the protected object must be a real dim-`g` AV even when `C(k)=∅`, so the unconditional `Pic⁰` construction is the right (and essentially forced) target.
14	- **Mathematical soundness**: PASS — and I verified the strategy's *new* iter-163 claim against the source: the **Albanese UP derivation** (Milne Prop 6.1/6.4) uses only Thm 3.2 + Cor 1.2 + Cor 1.5, with **no theorem of the cube**. Confirmed verbatim on PDF p.109–110: Prop 6.1's proof = "(I 3.2) shows [the rational map] to be a regular map ... (I 1.2) shows it is a homomorphism"; Prop 6.4 = "(I 1.5) shows there exist unique maps ... From (6.1) ...". Neither invokes §I.5.
15	- **Effort honesty**: reasonable — the strategy is candid that the Albanese UP shares Route C's Cor 1.5/1.2/Thm 3.2 infra and that the ~5100 LOC under-counts (true budget higher). One honest caveat the strategy already respects: J's *existence / projectivity / autoduality* (the FGA bulk, §III.1–5 + §I.6 + Thm 6.6's Poincaré sheaf) **does** rest on cube/square/seesaw — but the strategy never claims Route A is cube-free, only that the UP *derivation step* is, which is correct.
16	- **Verdict**: SOUND
17	
18	### Route: C — genus-0 rigidity completion via Milne §I.3 (COMMITTED iter-163)
19	
20	- **Goal-alignment**: PASS — genus-0 object is the trivial `J = Spec k`; the only content is "`ℙ¹→A` constant over `k̄`", which feeds `genusZeroWitness.key`.
21	- **Mathematical soundness**: PASS — cube-excision verified directly against `abelian-varieties.pdf`:
22	  - **Cor 1.2** (AV map `0↦0` is a hom): proof (PDF p.14) builds `φ(a,a')=α(a+a')−α(a)−α(a')`, collapses both axes, "and so `φ=0`" — by the **Rigidity Theorem 1.1**. No cube.
23	  - **Cor 1.5** (additivity `h=f∘p+g∘q`): proof (PDF p.14–15) "and so **the theorem** shows that `ψ=0`" — "the theorem" = Rigidity 1.1. This is exactly the project's proven Form-I Rigidity Lemma. No cube. (Requires `V,W` **complete**; `ℙ¹` is, so applicable.)
24	  - **Thm 3.2** = Thm 3.1 (valuative criterion on a DVR, PDF p.21–22) + **Lemma 3.3** (PDF p.22–23). Lemma 3.3 uses *Weil-divisor theory* on `V×V` (`div(f)₀/div(f)₁`) — but **no cube/seesaw/square**.
25	  - **Prop 3.9** (`ℙ¹→A` const, PDF p.24): Thm 3.2 + translate-to-`0↦0` + homomorphism + `𝔾_a/𝔾_m` incompatibility. No cube.
26	  - **Cube** (Thm 5.1, §I.5, PDF p.26) is *deferred/unproven* in Milne ("[this section needs to be rewritten to include the complete proof]") and its corollaries serve projectivity (§I.6), seesaw, dual AV, Poincaré sheaf — **none on the genus-0 or Albanese-UP path**. Excision is sound.
27	- **Phantom prerequisites**: none — `IsProper.of_valuativeCriterion` / `ValuativeCriterion` are the named Mathlib hooks for Thm 3.1; the divisor theory for Lemma 3.3 is correctly flagged as *absent* and to-build.
28	- **Effort honesty**: reasonable-to-optimistic — `~3000–5500` LOC for the base case is wide but plausible *given* that Lemma 3.3 needs surface divisor machinery with no Mathlib support; could under-count if full Weil-divisor theory is forced.
29	- **Verdict**: SOUND — with two accuracy notes for the planner (neither changes soundness or risk profile), see below.
30	
31	**Note C-1 (citation imprecision).** The genus-0 chain cites **Cor 1.2** for the homomorphism step, but Cor 1.2 is stated for maps **between abelian varieties** (`A→B`, both complete). The genus-0 base case needs the `𝔾_a→A` analogue: `φ(x,y)=f(x+y)−f(x)−f(y)` on `A¹×A¹`, killed by **Thm 3.4** (the *non-complete* product-rigidity), which itself routes through Thm 3.2. The correct citation for the genus-0 row is **Thm 3.4** (⟸ Thm 3.2 + Rigidity 1.1), not Cor 1.2. Cosmetic — Thm 3.2 is already listed and the surface gap is already the flagged risk.
32	
33	**Note C-2 (riskiest-piece identification is CORRECT — I checked the doubt).** I initially suspected Lemma 3.3 / `ℙ¹×ℙ¹` was mis-attributed to the genus-0 row (since the *source* `ℙ¹` is a curve, where Thm 3.1's curve case alone extends a rational map with no divisor theory). That suspicion is **wrong**: the genus-0 argument kills the additivity map `φ` which lives on `A¹×A¹`; to apply the complete Rigidity Lemma you must first extend `φ` to `ℙ¹×ℙ¹` (the `𝔾_a`-addition `A¹×A¹→A¹` does **not** extend to `ℙ¹×ℙ¹`), and that extension is precisely Thm 3.2's **surface** case = **Lemma 3.3**. So the surface divisor gap **is** genuinely on the genus-0 path, and the strategy's risk identification is correct. The "pointwise valuative side-step avoids building divisor theory?" open question is well-posed and correctly flagged OPEN — the valuative criterion gives extension *across one codim-1 point*, but "indeterminacy is *pure* codim 1 so codim-1-extension ⟹ everywhere-defined" is exactly Lemma 3.3's divisor-theoretic content. Not under-estimated.
34	
35	### Route: off-path fallbacks (c-hybrid / c-cube / b / a)
36	
37	- **Verdict**: SOUND — (c-cube) is correctly excised on a source-verified basis (cube on neither path); (c-hybrid) and (a) are correctly demoted for the char-`p` gap + from-scratch Ω-sheaf/cohomology/Frobenius cost; (b) correctly noted as coupling genus-0 to the Route A monster.

40	
41	- **Size**: 171 lines / 12,165 bytes — **marginally over budget** (~12 KB ceiling exceeded by ~165 B).
42	- **Headings**: PASS — exactly `## Goal`, `## Phases & estimations`, `## Routes`, `## Open strategic questions`, `## Mathlib gaps & new material`, in order.
43	- **Per-iter narrative detected**: **yes** — 12 `iter-NNN` occurrences embedded throughout, e.g. "DECIDED iter-163 = Route C", "PROVEN axiom-clean (iter-162)", "EXCISED iter-163", "REJECTED iter-163". The canonical skeleton forbids inline iteration references in STRATEGY.md (they belong in `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md`). The pointer "See `iter/iter-163/plan.md` for the full decision record" is fine; the inline `iter-162/163` tags are the violation.
44	- **Accumulation detected**: yes (mild) — the **char-`p` genus-0 rigidity** table row is now "(subsumed by genus-0 row)" / "— · 0" and could be dropped entirely; the "Off-path fallbacks" subsection documents the *excised* (c-cube) at length (excised routes belong in an iter sidecar, not occupying live strategy space).
45	- **Table discipline**: PASS — all six named columns present; LOC cells carry both figures. Minor: a couple of LOC cells substitute prose ("chain done, base-case 0/it") for the velocity figure.
46	- **Format verdict**: DRIFTED — core skeleton (headings + table) is intact; the pervasive `iter-NNN` narrative + the marginal size overflow + the subsumed char-`p` row are cleanup items the planner should resolve in-place this iter without a full restructure.
47	
48	## Must-fix-this-iter
49	
50	- Format (DRIFTED, not blocking but resolve in-place): purge the 12 inline `iter-NNN` tags (replace "DECIDED iter-163 = Route C" with "DECIDED: Route C", etc.), drop the subsumed char-`p` row, and trim the excised-(c-cube) prose into `iter/iter-163/plan.md` to bring the file back under 12 KB.
51	
52	## Overall verdict
53	
54	A fresh mathematician would approve the iter-163 resolution. The cube-excision is not hand-waved — I confirmed against Milne's text that every link in the genus-0 chain (Cor 1.2, Cor 1.5, Thm 3.2, Prop 3.9) and in the Route-A Albanese-UP derivation (Prop 6.1/6.4) rests on the already-proven Rigidity Lemma + the valuative criterion + divisor theory, and that the theorem of the cube is a *deferred, unproven* §I.5 result feeding only projectivity/autoduality. The committed Route C is sound and is genuinely the least-blocked path (valuative criterion present; differential route needs three from-scratch foundations). The single substantive risk — extending the additivity map across the `ℙ¹×ℙ¹` codim-1 indeterminacy without building full Weil-divisor theory (Lemma 3.3) — is correctly identified and honestly flagged OPEN; my attempt to find it mis-attributed failed (the surface extension really is on the genus-0 path). Two minor accuracy items (Cor 1.2 → Thm 3.4 citation; the genus-0 row arguably needs only Thm 3.4 + `Hom(𝔾_a,A)=0` + RR-bridge, with Cor 1.5 being a Route-A no-regret rather than strictly genus-0-essential) and a DRIFTED format (strip `iter-NNN`, trim accumulation) are the only cleanups. No route-level CHALLENGE or REJECT.

722	  \label{lem:rational_map_to_av_extends}
723	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.rationalMap_to_av_extends}
724	  % SOURCE: [Milne, Abelian Varieties], Theorem 3.2 (with Theorem 3.1 and Lemma 3.3), \S I.3, doc p.~16--18 (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF pages 22--24)
725	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "THEOREM 3.2. A rational map α:V ⇢ A from a nonsingular variety to an abelian
726	  % variety is defined on the whole of V."
727	  % SOURCE QUOTE (Thm 3.1, used in the proof): "THEOREM 3.1. A rational map φ:V ⇢ W from a
728	  % normal variety V to a complete variety W is defined on an open subset U of V whose
729	  % complement V∖U has codimension ≥ 2."
730	  % SOURCE QUOTE (Lemma 3.3, used in the proof): "LEMMA 3.3. Let φ:V ⇢ G be a rational map from
731	  % a nonsingular variety to a group variety. Then either φ is defined on all of V or the points
732	  % where it is not defined form a closed subset of pure codimension 1 in V (i.e., a finite
733	  % union of prime divisors)."
734	  \textit{Source: Milne, Abelian Varieties, Theorem~3.2 (with Theorem~3.1, Lemma~3.3).}
735	  A rational map $\alpha \colon V \dashrightarrow A$ from a nonsingular variety $V$ over
736	  $\bar k$ to an abelian variety $A$ is defined on the whole of $V$.
737	\end{lemma}
738	
739	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "PROOF. Combine Theorem 3.1 with the next lemma." [Milne, Thm 3.2, \S I.3,
740	% doc p.~17.]
741	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF (Thm 3.1, general case): "Let U be the largest subset on which φ is
742	% defined, and suppose that V∖U has codimension 1. Then there is a prime divisor Z in V∖U.
743	% Because V is normal, its associated local ring is a discrete valuation ring O_Z with field of
744	% fractions k(V). The map φ defines a morphism of schemes Spec(k(V)) → W, which the valuative
745	% criterion of properness (Hartshorne 1977, II 4.7) shows extends to a morphism Spec(O_Z) → W.
746	% This implies that φ has a representative defined on an open subset that meets Z in a nonempty
747	% set, which is a contradiction." [Milne, Thm 3.1, \S I.3, doc p.~17.]
748	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF (Lemma 3.3, the difference-map): "Define a rational map Φ:V×V ⇢ G,
749	% (x,y) ↦ φ(x)·φ(y)⁻¹. ... Thus φ is defined at x if and only if Φ is defined at (x,x). ...
750	% Suppose φ is not defined at x. Then for some f ∈ Im(Φ*), (x,x) ∈ div(f)₁, and clearly Φ is
751	% not defined at the points (y,y) ∈ Δ ∩ div(f)₁. This is a subset of pure codimension one in Δ,
752	% and when we identify it with a subset of V, it is a subset of V of codimension one passing
753	% through x on which φ is not defined." [Milne, Lemma 3.3, \S I.3, doc p.~17--18.]
754	\begin{proof}
755	  \textit{Source: Milne, Abelian Varieties, Theorem~3.2.}
756	  The statement is Theorem~3.1 combined with Lemma~3.3. By Theorem~3.1 (a consequence of the
757	  valuative criterion of properness, Mathlib \texttt{IsProper}/\texttt{ValuativeCriterion},
758	  applied at the discrete valuation ring of each codimension-$1$ point), a rational map from the
759	  normal $V$ to the complete $A$ is defined outside a closed set of codimension $\geq 2$. By
760	  Lemma~3.3, the indeterminacy locus of a rational map into a \emph{group} variety is either
761	  empty or pure of codimension $1$: forming the difference map
762	  $\Phi(x, y) = \varphi(x)\,\varphi(y)^{-1} \colon V \times V \dashrightarrow A$, one shows
763	  $\varphi$ is defined at $x$ iff $\Phi$ is defined at the diagonal point $(x, x)$, and an
764	  indeterminacy point produces a pure-codimension-$1$ component of the non-domain. The two
765	  facts are compatible only if the indeterminacy locus is empty; hence $\alpha$ is everywhere
766	  defined.
767	\end{proof}
768	
769	\begin{remark}
770	  \label{rmk:thm32_codim1_mathlib_gap}
771	  \textbf{Formalisation risk: the pure-codimension-$1$ step (Lemma 3.3) has no Mathlib
772	  Weil-divisor support.} Theorem~3.1's everywhere-extension-outside-codimension-$2$ is within
773	  reach of Mathlib's valuative criterion of properness
774	  (\texttt{ValuativeCriterion}/\texttt{IsProper.of\_valuativeCriterion}), applied at the
775	  discrete valuation ring $\mathcal O_{V, Z}$ of each codimension-$1$ point $Z$. But Lemma~3.3,
776	  which rules out codimension-$1$ indeterminacy for maps into a group variety, is proved in
777	  Milne via Weil-divisor theory ($\mathrm{div}(f)_0 - \mathrm{div}(f)_1$, prime divisors,
778	  tangent cones) for which Mathlib currently has \emph{no} usable API at this generality. This
779	  is the route's riskiest sub-build. An open question for the prover: whether a \emph{pointwise}
780	  valuative extension --- showing, at each codimension-$1$ point $Z$ separately, that the
781	  morphism $\Spec k(V) \to A$ extends over $\Spec \mathcal O_{V, Z}$ using only that $A$ is
782	  proper and a group (so the difference-map argument can be run \emph{locally} at $Z$ without
783	  globally building $\mathrm{div}(f)$) --- side-steps building divisor theory entirely. For the
784	  single application here ($V = \mathbb P^1$, already a morphism) the lemma is \emph{not even
785	  invoked} (see \cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}); it is needed only for the higher Albanese
786	  inputs of Route~A, so its sub-build can be deferred without blocking the genus-$0$ arm.
787	\end{remark}
788	
789	\begin{lemma}
790	[The $\mathbb G_a/\mathbb G_m$ incompatibility: a pointed $\mathbb P^1 \to A$ is constant (core of Milne Prop 3.9)]
791	  \label{lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial}
792	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.morphism_Ga_to_av_const}
793	  \uses{lem:av_regular_map_is_hom, lem:rational_map_to_av_extends}
794	  % SOURCE: [Milne, Abelian Varieties], Proposition 3.9, \S I.3, doc p.~19--20 (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF pages 25--26)
795	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "PROPOSITION 3.9. Every rational map A¹ ⇢ A or P¹ ⇢ A is constant."
796	  \textit{Source: Milne, Abelian Varieties, Proposition~3.9.}
797	  Let $A$ be an abelian variety over $\bar k$ and $f \colon \mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to A$ a
798	  morphism with $f(\infty) = 0_A$. Restricted to $\mathbb G_a = \mathbb A^1 = \mathbb P^1
799	  \setminus \{\infty\}$, the map $f$ is an additive-group homomorphism
800	  $f(x + y) = f(x) + f(y)$; restricted instead to $\mathbb G_m = \mathbb A^1 \setminus \{0\}$ it
801	  satisfies $f(xy) = f(x) + f(y) + c$ for a constant $c$. These two structures are incompatible
802	  unless $f$ is constant; hence $f$ is constant.
803	\end{lemma}
804	
805	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "PROOF. According to (3.2), α extends to a regular map on the whole of A¹.
806	% After composing α with a translation, we may suppose that α(0)=0. Then α is a homomorphism,
807	% α(x+y)=α(x)+α(y); all x,y ∈ A¹(k^al)=k^al. But A¹−{0} is also a group variety, and similarly,
808	% α(xy)=α(x)+α(y)+c; all x,y ∈ A¹(k^al)=k^al. This is absurd, unless α is constant." [Milne,
809	% Prop 3.9, \S I.3, doc p.~19--20.]
810	\begin{proof}
811	  \uses{lem:av_regular_map_is_hom, lem:rational_map_to_av_extends}
812	  \textit{Source: Milne, Abelian Varieties, Proposition~3.9.}
813	  Restrict $f$ to the affine line $\mathbb A^1 = \mathbb P^1 \setminus \{\infty\}$, regarded as
814	  the additive group variety $\mathbb G_a$ (it is everywhere defined there, and would extend
815	  even from a rational map by \cref{lem:rational_map_to_av_extends}). After the normalising
816	  translation $f(0) = 0_A$ is arranged, $f \colon \mathbb G_a \to A$ is a regular map of group
817	  varieties carrying $0$ to $0$, hence a homomorphism by \cref{lem:av_regular_map_is_hom}:
818	  \[ f(x + y) = f(x) + f(y), \qquad x, y \in \mathbb A^1(\bar k) = \bar k. \]
819	  But $\mathbb A^1 \setminus \{0\} = \mathbb G_m$ is \emph{also} a group variety, and the same
820	  homomorphism argument applied to the multiplicative structure gives
821	  \[ f(xy) = f(x) + f(y) + c, \qquad x, y \in \mathbb G_m(\bar k) = \bar k^\times, \]
822	  for a constant $c \in A$. The two relations are incompatible (an additive-group homomorphism
823	  $\mathbb G_a \to A$ that is simultaneously, up to translation, a multiplicative-group
824	  homomorphism $\mathbb G_m \to A$ on the overlap forces the image to be a single point), unless
825	  $f$ is constant. Hence $f$ is constant, with value $f(\infty)$ (the original base value before
826	  any translation). \qedhere
827	\end{proof}
828	
829	\section{Morphisms from $\mathbb P^1$ to an abelian variety are constant}
830	
831	\begin{proposition}
832

834	[A morphism $\mathbb P^1 \to A$ is constant]
835	  \label{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}
836	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const}
837	  \uses{lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial}
838	  % SOURCE: [Milne, Abelian Varieties], Proposition 3.10 (specialised to a single $\mathbb P^1$ factor), \S I.3, doc p.~20 (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF page 26)
839	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "PROPOSITION 3.10. Every rational map $\alpha \colon V \dashrightarrow A$
840	  % from a unirational variety to an abelian variety is constant."
841	  % SOURCE QUOTE (unirational, same page): "A variety $V$ over an algebraically closed field
842	  % is said to be unirational if there is a dominating rational map $\mathbb A^n
843	  % \dashrightarrow V$ with $n = \dim V$; equivalently, if $k(V)$ can be embedded into
844	  % $k(X_1, \ldots, X_n)$ (pure transcendental extension of $k$). A variety $V$ over an
845	  % arbitrary field $k$ is said to be unirational if $V_{k^{al}}$ is unirational."
846	  \textit{Source: Milne, Abelian Varieties, Proposition~3.10 (case $V = \mathbb P^1$).}
847	  Let $\bar k$ be algebraically closed and $A$ an abelian variety over $\bar k$. Then every
848	  morphism $f \colon \mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to A$ is constant: $f(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k})$ is a
849	  single $\bar k$-point.
850	\end{proposition}

I've confirmed the error. The blueprint claims (`rmk:thm32_codim1_mathlib_gap` L784–786, and `lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial` proof L813–817) that ℙ¹→A constant only needs Cor 1.2 directly and that the surface extension (Lemma 3.3) is *not* invoked for ℙ¹. That's mathematically wrong: the additive homomorphism property requires killing ψ(x,y)=f(x+y)−f(x)−f(y) on 𝔾ₐ×𝔾ₐ, which means extending ψ to the complete surface ℙ¹×ℙ¹ (Thm 3.4→3.2 surface case) and applying the Rigidity Lemma. As plan agent I'll correct these two blocks (source-backed) before the review.

through $x$ on which $\varphi$ is not defined) --- side-steps building divisor theory entirely.

  \textbf{This surface extension \emph{is} on the genus-$0$ critical path} (it cannot be deferred to
  Route~A). Although $f \colon \mathbb P^1 \to A$ is already a morphism, the homomorphism property of
  $f|_{\mathbb G_a}$ in \cref{lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial} is proved by collapsing the additive-defect
  map $\psi(x, y) = f(x + y) - f(x) - f(y)$, which is defined only on the affine surface
  $\mathbb G_a \times \mathbb G_a$; to apply the (complete-source) Rigidity Lemma one must first
  extend $\psi$ over the complete surface $\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb P^1$ (the addition
  $\mathbb A^1 \times \mathbb A^1 \to \mathbb A^1$ does \emph{not} extend to
  $\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb P^1$, so $\psi$ genuinely needs Theorem~3.2's \emph{surface} case
  = Lemma~3.3). This is exactly Milne's Theorem~3.4 (the non-complete product-rigidity), which
  routes through Theorem~3.2. So the codim-$1$ indeterminacy sub-build, or the pointwise valuative
  side-step that replaces it, is a genuine prerequisite of the genus-$0$ close and is the route's
  riskiest piece.
\end{remark}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{thm:rigidity_lemma, lem:hom_additivity_over_product, lem:rational_map_to_av_extends}
  \textit{Source: Milne, Abelian Varieties, Proposition~3.9.}
  Restrict $f$ to the affine line $\mathbb A^1 = \mathbb P^1 \setminus \{\infty\}$, regarded as
  the additive group variety $\mathbb G_a$, and arrange $f(0) = 0_A$ by a normalising translation.

  \textbf{$f|_{\mathbb G_a}$ is an additive homomorphism.} This is \emph{not} a direct instance of
  \cref{lem:av_regular_map_is_hom} (Milne Cor 1.2), which applies to maps \emph{between abelian
  varieties}: $\mathbb G_a$ is not complete. Instead form the additive-defect map
  \[ \psi(x, y) = f(x + y) - f(x) - f(y) \colon \mathbb G_a \times \mathbb G_a \to A, \]
  defined a priori only on the affine surface $\mathbb G_a \times \mathbb G_a$. Since the addition
  $\mathbb A^1 \times \mathbb A^1 \to \mathbb A^1$ does not extend to $\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb P^1$,
  $\psi$ is a rational map on the complete surface $\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb P^1$; by
  \cref{lem:rational_map_to_av_extends} (Thm 3.2, the surface case = Milne Thm 3.4, the riskiest
  sub-build --- see \cref{rmk:thm32_codim1_mathlib_gap}) it extends to a morphism
  $\bar\psi \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb P^1 \to A$. With $f(0) = 0$ one computes
  $\psi(x, 0) = 0 = \psi(0, y)$ on the axes, so $\bar\psi$ collapses both coordinate axes; by the
  Rigidity Lemma (\cref{thm:rigidity_lemma}; equivalently \cref{lem:hom_additivity_over_product})
  $\bar\psi \equiv 0$, i.e.
  \[ f(x + y) = f(x) + f(y), \qquad x, y \in \mathbb A^1(\bar k) = \bar k. \]
  But $\mathbb A^1 \setminus \{0\} = \mathbb G_m$ is \emph{also} a group variety, and the same
  additive-defect argument applied to the multiplicative structure gives

  morphisms-into-`A`) is missing. This is the crux of the Lean work and should be spelled out before

663	\begin{proof}
664	  \uses{thm:rigidity_lemma}
665	  \textit{Source: Milne, Abelian Varieties, Corollary~1.5.}
666	  Define $f := h|_{V \times \{w_0\}}$ and $g := h|_{\{v_0\} \times W}$, identifying
667	  $V \times \{w_0\} \cong V$ and $\{v_0\} \times W \cong W$; on points $f(v) = h(v, w_0)$ and
668	  $g(w) = h(v_0, w)$. Form the difference
669	  \[ \varphi := h - (f \circ p + g \circ q) \colon V \times W \to A, \qquad
670	     \varphi(v, w) = h(v, w) - h(v, w_0) - h(v_0, w) \]
671	  (the subtraction is in the group $A$, i.e.\ $\varphi$ is the composite of
672	  $(h, -(f \circ p + g \circ q))$ with the group law of $A$). Then $\varphi$ vanishes on both
673	  axes: $\varphi(V \times \{w_0\}) = 0 = \varphi(\{v_0\} \times W)$ (direct computation, using
674	  $h(v_0, w_0) = 0$). In particular $\varphi$ collapses the complete axis $V \times \{w_0\}$ to
675	  the single point $0 \in A$, so by the Rigidity Lemma (\cref{thm:rigidity_lemma}, with $V$
676	  complete and $V \times W$ geometrically irreducible) $\varphi$ factors through the projection
677	  $q$; but it also vanishes on $\{v_0\} \times W$, which is a section of $q$, so the factoring
678	  morphism is identically $0$, i.e.\ $\varphi \equiv 0$. Hence $h = f \circ p + g \circ q$.
679	  Uniqueness: restricting any such decomposition to the axes recovers $f$ and $g$ as the

I can see the Cor 1.5 block. Let me make the precise fixes. First, the Cor 1.5 group-structure detail (#4):

input.old_string:
$h \colon V \times W \to A$ with $h(v_0, w_0) = 0$ can be written uniquely as
  $h = f \circ p + g \circ q$, with morphisms $f \colon V \to A$, $g \colon W \to A$ satisfying
  $f(v_0) = 0$ and $g(w_0) = 0$.

input.new_string:
$h \colon V \times W \to A$ with $h(v_0, w_0) = 0$ can be written uniquely as
  $h = f \circ p + g \circ q$, with morphisms $f \colon V \to A$, $g \colon W \to A$ satisfying
  $f(v_0) = 0$ and $g(w_0) = 0$.

  \emph{Lean encoding.} In the project $A$ is a group object \texttt{[GrpObj A]} (not assumed
  commutative). The ``$+$'' here is the binary operation that \texttt{GrpObj} induces on each
  hom-set $\mathrm{Hom}(X, A)$: for $u, v \colon X \to A$, $u + v$ is the composite
  $X \xrightarrow{\langle u, v\rangle} A \times A \xrightarrow{\;\mathrm{mul}\;} A$ (and
  $f \circ p + g \circ q$ is built this way from the projections). The decomposition is therefore
  read \emph{multiplicatively} as $h = (f \circ p) \cdot (g \circ q)$ in the group
  $\mathrm{Hom}(V \times W, A)$, and does \emph{not} require $A$ commutative; commutativity of
  abelian varieties (Milne Cor 1.4, a separate consequence of \cref{lem:av_regular_map_is_hom})
  is needed only to rewrite the product symmetrically/additively, not for the statement or its
  proof. The prover should infer the hom-set group operation from the \texttt{GrpObj} instance,
  not from a \texttt{CommGrpObj} instance.
\end{lemma}

content:
6:The Jacobian is built as the Albanese object of $C$ in the category of smooth proper group schemes over $k$, and the construction stratifies by genus. For positive genus the witness \emph{object} is the abelian variety $\Pic^0_{C/k}$ produced by \textbf{Route~A (the Picard scheme via FGA representability)}: this is the project's committed critical path, and it is \emph{mandatory} for the positive-genus object regardless of any other route choice --- the object must be a genuine $g$-dimensional abelian variety even when $C(k) = \emptyset$ (see \cref{thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness} Route~A and \cref{def:positiveGenusWitness}). The FGA representability for $\Pic_{C/k}$ and its Hilbert/Quot prerequisites are the single largest Mathlib build-out the project gates on. For genus $0$ the witness object is instead the trivial scheme $\Spec k$, \textbf{decoupled} from Route~A's representability engine; the only remaining content in that arm is a rigidity \emph{statement}, the characteristic-free keystone \cref{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV} developed in \cref{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity} via the rigidity-lemma $+$ theorem-of-the-cube chain, \emph{not} the differential / Serre-duality argument. The over-$\bar k$ declaration \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} of \cref{chap:RigidityKbar}, which still carries $[\mathtt{CharZero}]$, is the retained fallback-(a) artifact and is \emph{not} the declaration the genus-$0$ witness consumes.
397:        \item[\textbf{(c) [committed; characteristic-free]}] \emph{Rigidity lemma $+$ theorem of the cube.} Over $\bar k$ a smooth proper geometrically irreducible genus-$0$ curve is isomorphic to $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ (\cref{prop:genusZero_curve_iso_P1}), and every morphism $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to A$ to an abelian variety is constant (\cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}); a fortiori the morphism $f$ is constant. The constancy of $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to A$ has two ingredients: the \emph{Rigidity Lemma} (\cref{thm:rigidity_lemma}, Mumford Form~I --- an elementary closed-map argument, cube-free) supplies the additive/multi-factor structure, while the single-curve base case is supplied by the \emph{theorem of the cube} (\cref{thm:theorem_of_the_cube}), the genuinely deep characteristic-free input forcing that an abelian variety carries no nonconstant image of $\mathbb P^1$ --- the Rigidity Lemma alone does \emph{not} suffice for the base case. Combining these yields the headline \cref{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV}. This chain uses \emph{neither} $H^0(C, \Omega_C) = 0$ / Serre duality \emph{nor} the Picard representability engine, and is valid in arbitrary characteristic; it is developed in \cref{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity}. It is the committed route for the genus-$0$ keystone; the over-$\bar k$ declaration \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} of \cref{chap:RigidityKbar} (which still carries $[\mathtt{CharZero}]$) is the fallback-(a) artifact, not consumed here.
417:      \item[\textbf{(C.2.g)}] \emph{Mathlib gap statement.} The keystone fact of Sub-step~C.2.d --- that every morphism from a smooth proper geometrically irreducible genus-$0$ curve over an algebraically closed field $\bar k$ to a positive-dimensional smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme over $\bar k$ is constant --- is \emph{not} present in current Mathlib (snapshot \texttt{b80f227}) in any form usable here. The project records the committed, characteristic-free form as the named declaration \cref{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV} of \cref{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity} (carrying $[\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]$ and \emph{no} $[\mathtt{CharZero}]$ hypothesis); the older over-$\bar k$ declaration \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} of \cref{chap:RigidityKbar}, whose base field still carries both $[\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]$ and $[\mathtt{CharZero}\ \bar k]$, is retained only as the fallback-(a) artifact. The \textbf{committed} proof route is route~(c) of C.2.d: the rigidity-lemma $+$ theorem-of-the-cube chain (\cref{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity}, headline \cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant} feeding \cref{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV}), which is characteristic-free and uses neither Serre duality nor the Picard engine. The differential decomposition into the shared cotangent-vanishing pile of \cref{sec:RigidityKbar_shared_pile} pieces (i)--(iii) --- (i) cotangent triviality of a group scheme over $\bar k$ via the functorial shear iso; (ii) scheme-level $\mathrm d f = 0 \Rightarrow$ $f$ factors through $\Spec \bar k$ via the \texttt{Differential.ContainConstants} typeclass plus the iter-125-refactored \texttt{Scheme.Over.ext\_of\_eqOnOpen}; (iii) characteristic-$p$ handling via the intrinsic absolute Frobenius $F_X$ --- is the \textbf{fallback} route~(a), off the critical path but retained in the tree (each piece an upstream Mathlib gap-fill in its own right; see \cref{chap:RigidityKbar}). When $g = \dim A = 0$ the statement is vacuous and the declaration's conclusion is automatic. The reduction step C.2.b reuses the project's already-formalised Theorem~\ref{thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen} (after the iter-125 refactor, this is \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Over.ext\_of\_eqOnOpen}, stated at the scheme level: source reduced and geometrically irreducible, target separated); the promotion step C.2.e uses standard reduced-source / separated-target lemmas, partially in Mathlib. The descent step C.2.f --- from $\bar k$ back to the base field $k$ --- has a genuinely short \emph{final} move (the epi-cancellation via \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.epi\_of\_flat\_of\_surjective}, already in Mathlib in \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Flat}), but the descent as a whole is \emph{not} a $\sim 2$-line step: it is a multi-iteration base-change sub-build, because neither this project nor Mathlib provides a base-change functor $\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec k) \to \mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec \bar k)$, and the instance transfer, genus-stability, and base-change-square identities (C.2.f items (a)--(d)) must all be built before the closing epi-cancellation applies.
434:    \item[\textbf{($\gamma$)}] \emph{The rigidity statement for morphisms from a smooth proper geometrically irreducible genus-$0$ curve to a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme, over an algebraically closed field $\bar k$.} The genus-$0$ arm needs \emph{only} this rigidity \emph{statement}, \textbf{decoupled} from both the cotangent-vanishing pile and the Picard representability of ($\alpha$): its witness object is the trivial $\Spec k$, and the statement is the project's named, characteristic-free declaration \cref{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV} (\cref{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity}), carrying $[\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]$ and \emph{no} $[\mathtt{CharZero}]$ hypothesis (the older \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} of \cref{chap:RigidityKbar}, still carrying both $[\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]$ and $[\mathtt{CharZero}\ \bar k]$, is the retained fallback-(a) artifact). The \textbf{committed} proof route is the characteristic-free rigidity-lemma $+$ theorem-of-the-cube chain of \cref{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity} (route~(c): headline \cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}, Milne Proposition~3.10 specialised to $\mathbb P^1$), which uses neither Serre duality nor representability. The differential decomposition into the shared cotangent-vanishing pile pieces (i)+(ii)+(iii) of \cref{sec:RigidityKbar_shared_pile} --- cotangent triviality of a group scheme over $\bar k$ via the functorial shear iso; scheme-level $\mathrm d f = 0 \Rightarrow$ $f$ factors through $\Spec \bar k$ via the \texttt{Differential.ContainConstants} typeclass; characteristic-$p$ handling via the intrinsic absolute Frobenius $F_X$ --- is the \textbf{fallback} route~(a), off the critical path but retained in the tree. The genus-$0$ arm of the witness existence is packaged as \cref{def:genusZeroWitness} (\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.genusZeroWitness}), which over a general base field $k$ \emph{descends} the over-$\bar k$ rigidity conclusion to $k$: for each $k$-rational marked point $P \in C(k)$ it base-changes the data $(C, A, f, P)$ along $\Spec \bar k \to \Spec k$ (genus is stable under base change for a geometrically irreducible curve, so $C_{\bar k}$ is again a genus-$0$ curve), applies \cref{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV} over $\bar k$ to conclude $f_{\bar k}$ is constant, then descends the morphism equality back to $k$ along the faithfully-flat surjection $\Spec \bar k \to \Spec k$ (Sub-step~C.2.f, whose closing move is \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.epi\_of\_flat\_of\_surjective}). The $C(k) = \emptyset$ branch is vacuously true (Brauer--Severi conics over $\mathbb Q$). Independently of ($\alpha$), establishing ($\gamma$) together with the descent step (C.2.f --- a multi-iteration base-change sub-build, not a $\sim 2$-line step; see the re-costing there) lets the genus-$0$ part of the witness be discharged unconditionally via \cref{def:genusZeroWitness}; the higher-genus part is packaged as \cref{def:positiveGenusWitness} (\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.positiveGenusWitness}, iter-134 scaffold) whose body closure is gated on Route A ($\alpha$). Per the iter-135 body restructure of \cref{thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness}, the witness existence is now a case-split $\mathtt{by\_cases}\ h : \genus\,C = 0$ delegating to \cref{def:genusZeroWitness} and \cref{def:positiveGenusWitness} respectively, converting the formerly inline \texttt{sorry} into honest delegation to the two named scaffolds.
448:\subsection{Route (c): rigidity via the theorem of the cube (committed genus-$0$ proof route)}
453:The chain of \cref{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity} has four links. The \emph{Rigidity Lemma} (\cref{thm:rigidity_lemma}, Mumford Form~I) is an elementary properness/closed-map argument --- cube-free and cohomology-free --- and supplies the additive/multi-factor structure. The \emph{theorem of the cube} (\cref{thm:theorem_of_the_cube}) enters as the genuinely deep, characteristic-free input supplying the single-curve base case that an abelian variety carries no nonconstant image of $\mathbb P^1$; the cube is \textbf{required} here --- the Rigidity Lemma alone does \emph{not} establish that base case. Combining the two gives that every morphism $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to A$ is constant (\cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}); composing with the genus-$0$ classification $C \cong \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ over $\bar k$ (\cref{prop:genusZero_curve_iso_P1}) yields \cref{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV}. This chain uses \emph{neither} $H^0(C, \Omega_C) = 0$ / Serre duality \emph{nor} the FGA $\Pic$ representability engine, so the genus-$0$ arm is fully \textbf{decoupled} from Route~A; the differential / Serre-duality argument survives only as the off-critical-path fallback~(a) of \cref{chap:RigidityKbar}.
481:  \paragraph{Body closure status.} The Lean body of \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.genusZeroWitness} (in \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean}) is currently \texttt{sorry}; the closure is gated on the body of the upstream keystone \cref{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV} (M2.a; in \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean}, imported by \texttt{Jacobian.lean}), whose \textbf{committed} proof route is route~(c) --- the characteristic-free rigidity-lemma $+$ theorem-of-the-cube chain of \cref{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity}, which is decoupled from both Route~A representability and Serre duality. (The over-$\bar k$ declaration \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} of \cref{chap:RigidityKbar} is the retained fallback-(a) artifact, not the keystone consumed here.) The differential decomposition into pieces (i)+(ii)+(iii) of the shared cotangent-vanishing pile (\cref{sec:RigidityKbar_shared_pile}) is the retained \emph{fallback} route~(a), off the critical path. (The chart-algebra envelope of the fallback supplies only the converse implication $df = 0 \Rightarrow$ constant, and carries a characteristic-$p$ gap; the committed route~(c) avoids that gap entirely.)
523:  \item \textbf{Layer I --- direct definition.} The Jacobian of $C$ is defined uniformly as the underlying scheme of the Albanese witness provided by Theorem~\ref{thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness}, carrying the four properties of Theorems~\ref{thm:Jacobian_grpObj}--\ref{thm:Jacobian_geomIrred} (each obtained by projection from the witness). The genus-$0$ case is included: the witness object is the trivial $\Spec k$, and the witness's universal property carries only the rigidity \emph{statement} for morphisms from a smooth proper geometrically irreducible genus-$0$ curve to a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme --- \textbf{decoupled} from the Picard representability of Route~A. This rigidity is established over the algebraic closure $\bar k = \mathrm{AlgebraicClosure}\,k$ via the characteristic-free keystone \cref{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV} of the upstream \cref{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity} (carrying $[\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]$, no $[\mathtt{CharZero}]$), whose proof route is the rigidity-lemma $+$ theorem-of-the-cube chain of that chapter (route~(c)); the over-$\bar k$ \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} of \cref{chap:RigidityKbar} is the retained fallback-(a) artifact (the differential / Serre-duality route, shared cotangent-vanishing pile pieces (i)+(ii)+(iii)). The conclusion over a general base field $k$ is then obtained by \emph{descending} the over-$\bar k$ morphism equality along the faithfully-flat surjection $\Spec \bar k \to \Spec k$ (whose closing epi-cancellation is \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.epi\_of\_flat\_of\_surjective}; the surrounding base change is a multi-iteration sub-build, see Sub-step~C.2.f). The genus-$0$ arm is packaged as the explicit Lean construction \cref{def:genusZeroWitness}: the underlying scheme is the terminal object $\Spec k$, the universal morphism in the $C(k) \neq \emptyset$ branch is the unique terminal map \texttt{toUnit}\,$C$, and the universal factorisation reduces to \cref{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV} over $\bar k$ followed by descent to $k$.

`Jacobian.tex` describes route (c) as "rigidity-lemma + theorem-of-the-cube chain" in **8 places** (broken `\cref`s + a section heading + stale "cube required" claims) — that's a coherent-rewrite job for a writer, and it's a must-fix-this-iter. It's a *separate* chapter (gates `Jacobian.lean`, not my Cor 1.5 target in `AVR.lean`). I'll dispatch a writer for `Jacobian.tex` and, in parallel, a scoped fast-path re-review of `AVR.tex` to confirm my two fixes clear the gate for the Cor 1.5 prover target.

input.command:
cd /home/archon/FormalizationProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
cat > .archon/logs/iter-163/blueprint-writer-jacobian-cube-purge-directive.md <<'EOF'
# Blueprint-writer directive (iter-163) — purge the theorem-of-the-cube narrative from Jacobian.tex (align route (c) with Route C = Milne §I.3)

## Chapter to edit (ONLY this file)
`blueprint/src/chapters/Jacobian.tex`

## Why
The genus-0 base-case route was decided this iter = **Route C (Milne §I.3 rigidity completion)**,
which is **cube-free**: reading Milne §I.3 (Prop 3.9/3.10) showed "ℙ¹→A constant" is derived from
the Rigidity Lemma (= the project's proven `thm:rigidity_lemma`) via Cor 1.5 (additivity) + Cor 1.2
(AV maps are homs) + Thm 3.2 (rational-map extension, valuative criterion) + the 𝔾_a/𝔾_m
incompatibility — with NO theorem of the cube. The cube block was DELETED from
`AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex` this iter (the label `thm:theorem_of_the_cube` no longer exists).
`Jacobian.tex` still describes route (c) throughout as the "rigidity-lemma + theorem-of-the-cube
chain" and contains broken `\cref{thm:theorem_of_the_cube}` references and the now-FALSE claim that
"the cube is required" / "the Rigidity Lemma alone does not suffice for the base case".

## Required fixes (all occurrences — there are ~8; grep for `theorem_of_the_cube`, `theorem of the cube`, `cube`)
Replace the cube narrative with the correct Route C / Milne §I.3 description everywhere it appears,
specifically:
- **Remove every `\cref{thm:theorem_of_the_cube}`** (the label is deleted — these are broken refs).
- **Line ~6 (chapter intro):** change "via the rigidity-lemma + theorem-of-the-cube chain" to
  "via the Milne §I.3 rigidity chain (Rigidity Lemma + additivity Cor 1.5 + Cor 1.2 + rational-map
  extension), cube-free".
- **Item (c) at line ~397** and **the section heading at line ~448**
  (`\subsection{Route (c): rigidity via the theorem of the cube ...}`) and **its body at line ~453**:
  rewrite so route (c) is "rigidity via Milne §I.3 (cube-free)". The corrected mathematical content:
  the base case "ℙ¹→A constant" is NOT supplied by the theorem of the cube; it is supplied by the
  𝔾_a/𝔾_m incompatibility argument (Milne Prop 3.9), in which the additive-defect map
  ψ(x,y)=f(x+y)−f(x)−f(y) on 𝔾_a×𝔾_a is extended to the complete surface ℙ¹×ℙ¹ (Milne Thm 3.4 →
  Thm 3.2) and collapsed by the Rigidity Lemma. Cite `\cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}` and
  `\cref{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV}` (these labels still exist in AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex);
  you MAY also cite the new sub-lemma labels `lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial`, `lem:hom_additivity_over_product`,
  `lem:rational_map_to_av_extends` from that chapter.
- **Lines ~417, ~434, ~481, ~523:** replace each "rigidity-lemma + theorem-of-the-cube chain" phrase
  with "Milne §I.3 rigidity chain (cube-free)"; keep the surrounding correct statements (decoupling
  from Route A, the [IsAlgClosed]/no-[CharZero] hypotheses, the fallback-(a) artifact note, the
  descent C.2.f narrative) intact — only the cube phrasing is wrong.
- Note the riskiest sub-build is now the surface rational-map-extension gap (Milne Lemma 3.3,
  pure-codim-1 indeterminacy on ℙ¹×ℙ¹ — no Mathlib Weil-divisor theory), per
  `AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex` `rmk:thm32_codim1_mathlib_gap`. Do NOT re-introduce any cube claim.

## Out of scope
- Do NOT touch `AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex` (already corrected this iter).
- Do NOT change protected signatures (`thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness`, `def:genusZeroWitness`,
  `def:positiveGenusWitness`, `Jacobian.*`) — only the prose describing route (c).
- Marker management (`\leanok`/`\mathlibok`): do not add/remove. You may keep/adjust `\lean{}` hints.

## Citation discipline
The cube-free derivation is sourced from Milne §I.3 (already quoted verbatim in
`AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex`); you do NOT need new verbatim quotes here (Jacobian.tex is a routing
narrative). If you state a NEW sourced claim, follow citation discipline (read
`references/abelian-varieties.pdf` via pypdf). Report any "Strategy-modifying findings".
EOF

cat > .archon/logs/iter-163/blueprint-reviewer-avr-fastpath-directive.md <<'EOF'
# Blueprint-reviewer directive (iter-163, FAST-PATH) — scoped re-review of AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex ONLY

This is a same-iter fast-path re-review after the plan agent fixed the two must-fix items you flagged
in `blueprint-reviewer-iter163` for `AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex`. **Scope: ONLY
`blueprint/src/chapters/AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex`.** Ignore all other chapters this pass (other
chapters, e.g. `Jacobian.tex`, are being edited concurrently by another writer and are NOT in scope).

## The two must-fix items you flagged (verify they are now resolved)
1. **(was must-fix #3, `correct`)** `prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant` proof previously cited
   `\cref{lem:av_regular_map_is_hom}` (Cor 1.2) for the 𝔾_a homomorphism step — an internal
   contradiction with `lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial` (which states Cor 1.2 is inapplicable to the
   non-complete 𝔾_a). The plan agent rewrote the "𝔾_a/𝔾_m argument" paragraph to invoke
   `lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial` directly and to state explicitly that Cor 1.2 is NOT used there.
2. **(was must-fix #4, `complete`)** `lem:hom_additivity_over_product` (Cor 1.5) omitted where the
   `+`/group structure on `Hom(−,A)` comes from. The plan agent added a "Lean encoding" paragraph:
   `A` is `[GrpObj A]` (not commutative); `+` is the GrpObj-induced hom-set operation
   (`⟨u,v⟩ ≫ mul`); the decomposition is read multiplicatively `h=(f∘p)·(g∘q)` and does NOT require
   commutativity (Milne Cor 1.4 is needed only to write it symmetrically).

## Your job
Re-audit AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex for completeness + correctness. Report:
- Whether both must-fix items are now resolved.
- Whether the chapter is `complete: true` AND `correct: true` with NO remaining must-fix-this-iter
  finding (the proven Rigidity-Lemma chain blocks should still read untouched + correct; the §I.3
  chain `\uses` graph forward-acyclic; no edge to the deleted `thm:theorem_of_the_cube`).
- Explicit HARD GATE verdict: does AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex clear the gate for a prover on
  `lem:hom_additivity_over_product` (Cor 1.5) and `lem:av_regular_map_is_hom` (Cor 1.2) THIS iter?
EOF
echo "both directives written"

=== Jacobian.tex writer report tail ===

These are plain `\cref` prose references (not `\uses{}` dependency edges), consistent with the
existing routing-narrative style of this chapter.

## References consulted

=== context of remaining 'cube' mentions ===
397:        \item[\textbf{(c) [committed; characteristic-free]}] \emph{Milne \S I.3 rigidity chain (cube-free).} Over $\bar k$ a smooth proper geometrically irreducible genus-$0$ curve is isomorphic to $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ (\cref{prop:genusZero_curve_iso_P1}), and every morphism $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to A$ to an abelian variety is constant (\cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}); a fortiori the morphism $f$ is constant. The constancy of $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to A$ is \emph{not} supplied by the theorem of the cube. The base case is the $\mathbb G_a/\mathbb G_m$ group-law incompatibility (Milne Proposition~3.9, \cref{lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial}): the additive-defect map $\psi(x,y) = f(x+y) - f(x) - f(y)$ on $\mathbb G_a \times \mathbb G_a$ is extended to the complete surface $\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb P^1$ (rational-map extension \cref{lem:rational_map_to_av_extends}, Milne Thm~3.2/3.4) and then collapsed by the \emph{Rigidity Lemma} (\cref{thm:rigidity_lemma}, Mumford Form~I --- an elementary closed-map argument), forcing $f|_{\mathbb G_a}$ to be an additive homomorphism; the same argument on $\mathbb G_m$ produces a multiplicative relation incompatible with it unless $f$ is constant. The additivity Corollary~1.5 (\cref{lem:hom_additivity_over_product}) packages the same collapse over a product. Combining with the genus-$0$ classification yields the headline \cref{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV}. This chain uses \emph{neither} the theorem of the cube, \emph{nor} $H^0(C, \Omega_C) = 0$ / Serre duality, \emph{nor} the Picard representability engine, and is valid in arbitrary characteristic; it is developed in \cref{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity}. It is the committed route for the genus-$0$ keystone; the over-$\bar k$ declaration \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} of \cref{chap:RigidityKbar} (which still carries $[\mathtt{CharZero}]$) is the fallback-(a) artifact, not consumed here.
417:      \item[\textbf{(C.2.g)}] \emph{Mathlib gap statement.} The keystone fact of Sub-step~C.2.d --- that every morphism from a smooth proper geometrically irreducible genus-$0$ curve over an algebraically closed field $\bar k$ to a positive-dimensional smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme over $\bar k$ is constant --- is \emph{not} present in current Mathlib (snapshot \texttt{b80f227}) in any form usable here. The project records the committed, characteristic-free form as the named declaration \cref{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV} of \cref{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity} (carrying $[\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]$ and \emph{no} $[\mathtt{CharZero}]$ hypothesis); the older over-$\bar k$ declaration \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} of \cref{chap:RigidityKbar}, whose base field still carries both $[\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]$ and $[\mathtt{CharZero}\ \bar k]$, is retained only as the fallback-(a) artifact. The \textbf{committed} proof route is route~(c) of C.2.d: the Milne \S I.3 rigidity chain (cube-free) (\cref{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity}, headline \cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant} feeding \cref{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV}), which is characteristic-free and uses neither the theorem of the cube, nor Serre duality, nor the Picard engine. The differential decomposition into the shared cotangent-vanishing pile of \cref{sec:RigidityKbar_shared_pile} pieces (i)--(iii) --- (i) cotangent triviality of a group scheme over $\bar k$ via the functorial shear iso; (ii) scheme-level $\mathrm d f = 0 \Rightarrow$ $f$ factors through $\Spec \bar k$ via the \texttt{Differential.ContainConstants} typeclass plus the iter-125-refactored \texttt{Scheme.Over.ext\_of\_eqOnOpen}; (iii) characteristic-$p$ handling via the intrinsic absolute Frobenius $F_X$ --- is the \textbf{fallback} route~(a), off the critical path but retained in the tree (each piece an upstream Mathlib gap-fill in its own right; see \cref{chap:RigidityKbar}). When $g = \dim A = 0$ the statement is vacuous and the declaration's conclusion is automatic. The reduction step C.2.b reuses the project's already-formalised Theorem~\ref{thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen} (after the iter-125 refactor, this is \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Over.ext\_of\_eqOnOpen}, stated at the scheme level: source reduced and geometrically irreducible, target separated); the promotion step C.2.e uses standard reduced-source / separated-target lemmas, partially in Mathlib. The descent step C.2.f --- from $\bar k$ back to the base field $k$ --- has a genuinely short \emph{final} move (the epi-cancellation via \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.epi\_of\_flat\_of\_surjective}, already in Mathlib in \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Flat}), but the descent as a whole is \emph{not} a $\sim 2$-line step: it is a multi-iteration base-change sub-build, because neither this project nor Mathlib provides a base-change functor $\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec k) \to \mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec \bar k)$, and the instance transfer, genus-stability, and base-change-square identities (C.2.f items (a)--(d)) must all be built before the closing epi-cancellation applies.
434:    \item[\textbf{($\gamma$)}] \emph{The rigidity statement for morphisms from a smooth proper geometrically irreducible genus-$0$ curve to a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme, over an algebraically closed field $\bar k$.} The genus-$0$ arm needs \emph{only} this rigidity \emph{statement}, \textbf{decoupled} from both the cotangent-vanishing pile and the Picard representability of ($\alpha$): its witness object is the trivial $\Spec k$, and the statement is the project's named, characteristic-free declaration \cref{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV} (\cref{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity}), carrying $[\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]$ and \emph{no} $[\mathtt{CharZero}]$ hypothesis (the older \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} of \cref{chap:RigidityKbar}, still carrying both $[\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]$ and $[\mathtt{CharZero}\ \bar k]$, is the retained fallback-(a) artifact). The \textbf{committed} proof route is the characteristic-free Milne \S I.3 rigidity chain (cube-free) of \cref{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity} (route~(c): headline \cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}, Milne Proposition~3.10 specialised to $\mathbb P^1$), which uses neither the theorem of the cube, nor Serre duality, nor representability. The differential decomposition into the shared cotangent-vanishing pile pieces (i)+(ii)+(iii) of \cref{sec:RigidityKbar_shared_pile} --- cotangent triviality of a group scheme over $\bar k$ via the functorial shear iso; scheme-level $\mathrm d f = 0 \Rightarrow$ $f$ factors through $\Spec \bar k$ via the \texttt{Differential.ContainConstants} typeclass; characteristic-$p$ handling via the intrinsic absolute Frobenius $F_X$ --- is the \textbf{fallback} route~(a), off the critical path but retained in the tree. The genus-$0$ arm of the witness existence is packaged as \cref{def:genusZeroWitness} (\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.genusZeroWitness}), which over a general base field $k$ \emph{descends} the over-$\bar k$ rigidity conclusion to $k$: for each $k$-rational marked point $P \in C(k)$ it base-changes the data $(C, A, f, P)$ along $\Spec \bar k \to \Spec k$ (genus is stable under base change for a geometrically irreducible curve, so $C_{\bar k}$ is again a genus-$0$ curve), applies \cref{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV} over $\bar k$ to conclude $f_{\bar k}$ is constant, then descends the morphism equality back to $k$ along the faithfully-flat surjection $\Spec \bar k \to \Spec k$ (Sub-step~C.2.f, whose closing move is \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.epi\_of\_flat\_of\_surjective}). The $C(k) = \emptyset$ branch is vacuously true (Brauer--Severi conics over $\mathbb Q$). Independently of ($\alpha$), establishing ($\gamma$) together with the descent step (C.2.f --- a multi-iteration base-change sub-build, not a $\sim 2$-line step; see the re-costing there) lets the genus-$0$ part of the witness be discharged unconditionally via \cref{def:genusZeroWitness}; the higher-genus part is packaged as \cref{def:positiveGenusWitness} (\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.positiveGenusWitness}, iter-134 scaffold) whose body closure is gated on Route A ($\alpha$). Per the iter-135 body restructure of \cref{thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness}, the witness existence is now a case-split $\mathtt{by\_cases}\ h : \genus\,C = 0$ delegating to \cref{def:genusZeroWitness} and \cref{def:positiveGenusWitness} respectively, converting the formerly inline \texttt{sorry} into honest delegation to the two named scaffolds.
453:The chain of \cref{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity} is \textbf{cube-free}: the theorem of the cube is \emph{not} on the genus-$0$ path (it enters Milne's theory only later, for projectivity, the seesaw principle, dual abelian varieties, and the Poincar\'e sheaf). It has four links, all resting on Milne \S I.3. The \emph{Rigidity Lemma} (\cref{thm:rigidity_lemma}, Mumford Form~I) is an elementary properness/closed-map argument --- cohomology-free --- and supplies the collapse of a complete-factor map. From it, the \emph{additive decomposition} Corollary~1.5 (\cref{lem:hom_additivity_over_product}) and \emph{Corollary~1.2} that a pointed regular map of abelian varieties is a homomorphism follow directly. The single-curve base case --- that every morphism $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to A$ is constant --- is supplied \emph{not} by the cube but by the $\mathbb G_a/\mathbb G_m$ group-law incompatibility (Milne Proposition~3.9, \cref{lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial}): the additive-defect map $\psi(x,y) = f(x+y) - f(x) - f(y)$, defined a priori only on the affine surface $\mathbb G_a \times \mathbb G_a$, is extended to the complete surface $\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb P^1$ by the rational-map-extension Theorem~3.2/3.4 (\cref{lem:rational_map_to_av_extends}) and then collapsed by the Rigidity Lemma, forcing $f|_{\mathbb G_a}$ to be an additive homomorphism; the parallel relation $f(xy) = f(x) + f(y) + c$ on $\mathbb G_m$ is incompatible with it unless $f$ is constant. This gives \cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}; composing with the genus-$0$ classification $C \cong \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ over $\bar k$ (\cref{prop:genusZero_curve_iso_P1}) yields \cref{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV}. This chain uses \emph{neither} the theorem of the cube, \emph{nor} $H^0(C, \Omega_C) = 0$ / Serre duality, \emph{nor} the FGA $\Pic$ representability engine, so the genus-$0$ arm is fully \textbf{decoupled} from Route~A; the differential / Serre-duality argument survives only as the off-critical-path fallback~(a) of \cref{chap:RigidityKbar}. The route's \textbf{riskiest sub-build} is the surface rational-map-extension step (Milne Lemma~3.3, pure-codimension-$1$ indeterminacy on $\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb P^1$, for which Mathlib has no Weil-divisor theory; see \cref{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity} \cref{rmk:thm32_codim1_mathlib_gap}), which is genuinely on the genus-$0$ critical path and cannot be deferred to Route~A.
481:  \paragraph{Body closure status.} The Lean body of \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.genusZeroWitness} (in \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean}) is currently \texttt{sorry}; the closure is gated on the body of the upstream keystone \cref{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV} (M2.a; in \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean}, imported by \texttt{Jacobian.lean}), whose \textbf{committed} proof route is route~(c) --- the characteristic-free Milne \S I.3 rigidity chain (cube-free) of \cref{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity}, which is decoupled from the theorem of the cube, Route~A representability, and Serre duality. (The over-$\bar k$ declaration \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} of \cref{chap:RigidityKbar} is the retained fallback-(a) artifact, not the keystone consumed here.) The differential decomposition into pieces (i)+(ii)+(iii) of the shared cotangent-vanishing pile (\cref{sec:RigidityKbar_shared_pile}) is the retained \emph{fallback} route~(a), off the critical path. (The chart-algebra envelope of the fallback supplies only the converse implication $df = 0 \Rightarrow$ constant, and carries a characteristic-$p$ gap; the committed route~(c) avoids that gap entirely.)

All 5 remaining "cube" mentions in Jacobian.tex are now **correct** ("not supplied by the cube", "cube-free", etc.) — no broken `\cref`. Both chapters are Route-C-consistent. The gate clears for the Cor 1.5 / Cor 1.2 prover targets. Let me read `rigidity_lemma`'s exact signature so the prover objective is accurate.

input.content:
# Iter-163 plan-agent run

## Headline outcome

iter-163 is the **base-case route DECISION iter** mandated as the binding iter-163 task. The
Rigidity-Lemma chain closed axiom-clean in iter-162, so the prior Current-Objective (Step 1) was
done and the gating question was: how to finish the genus-0 base case `ℙ¹→A constant`. This iter
resolved it from primary sources and pivoted the blueprint accordingly.

**The decision: commit Route C (Milne *Abelian Varieties* §I.3 rigidity completion); EXCISE the
theorem of the cube; demote the differential/Frobenius route to off-path fallback.**

This corrects a multi-iter MISCONCEPTION (iter-156→162): the blueprint had asserted "ℙ¹→A constant
rests on the theorem of the cube." Reading Milne directly showed this is **false**.

## What I read (the binding gating verification)

From `references/abelian-varieties.pdf` (Milne), verbatim:
- **§I.1 Rigidity Thm 1.1** (PDF p.14–15): IS the project's proven Mumford Form-I `rigidity_lemma`;
  its proof uses only completeness + "complete connected → affine is constant" — NO cube.
- **§I.1 Cor 1.2 / Cor 1.5** (PDF p.15): AV-maps-are-homs + additivity, both from Rigidity 1.1.
- **§I.3 Thm 3.1/3.2 + Lemma 3.3** (PDF p.22–24): rational-map extension via the valuative criterion
  + pure-codim-1 indeterminacy (Weil divisors on a surface).
- **§I.3 Prop 3.9/3.10** (PDF p.25–26): `ℙ¹→A constant` = Thm 3.2 + Cor 1.2 + the `𝔾_a/𝔾_m`
  incompatibility — NO cube.
- **§III.6 Prop 6.1/6.4** (PDF p.110–111): the Albanese UP Route A needs is ALSO cube-free (Thm 3.2
  + Cor 1.2/1.5). The cube (§I.5) feeds only projectivity/seesaw/dual-AV/Poincaré — neither path.

## Subagent verdicts (absorbed)

| Subagent | Slug | Verdict | Absorption |
|---|---|---|---|
| mathlib-analogist | route-support | **Route R (rigidity) ≫ Route H (differential)** | Route C keystone (valuative criterion) is IN Mathlib (`IsProper.of_valuativeCriterion`); Route H needs THREE from-scratch foundations (Ω-sheaf, coherent cohomology + Serre H⁰ = "single largest gap", scheme Frobenius). Decisive for committing Route C. `analogies/route-support.md`. |
| strategy-critic | route-c-decision | **SOUND (all routes), no CHALLENGE/REJECT** | Independently re-verified the cube-excision against Milne's text (every §I.3 link + the Albanese UP). Confirmed the surface gap (Lemma 3.3 on `ℙ¹×ℙ¹`) IS genuinely on the genus-0 path (Note C-2). Must-fix = STRATEGY format DRIFTED (iter-NNN tags, char-p row, fallback prose) — **addressed in-place** (160 lines / 10.9 KB). |
| blueprint-writer | route-c | COMPLETE | Cube `\section`+`rmk:cube_is_load_bearing` removed; §I.3 chain added (Cor 1.5, Cor 1.2, Thm 3.2, Prop 3.9 core + 2 remarks); Prop 3.9 rewritten. Strategy-finding #2 (Lemma 3.3 "not needed for ℙ¹") was **WRONG** — see below. |
| blueprint-reviewer | iter163 | **partial** (4 small must-fix) | Cube-excision mathematically correct; Rigidity chain intact. Must-fix: (1) prop:morphism_P1 still cited Cor 1.2 for 𝔾_a step; (2/3) Jacobian.tex stale cube refs; (4) Cor 1.5 group-structure provenance. |
| blueprint-writer | jacobian-cube-purge | COMPLETE | Jacobian.tex route-(c) narrative aligned to cube-free Milne §I.3 (8 spots); no broken `\cref`; protected sigs untouched. |
| blueprint-reviewer | avr-fastpath | **HARD GATE CLEARS** | AVR.tex `complete:true`+`correct:true`, 0 must-fix after my 2 fixes; gate clears for Cor 1.5 + Cor 1.2 this iter. |

## A blueprint error I caught and corrected (writer finding #2 was wrong)

The route-c writer claimed "for V=ℙ¹ the map is already a morphism, so Lemma 3.3 / the surface
extension is NOT invoked — defer it to Route A." This is **mathematically wrong** (corroborated by
strategy-critic Note C-2): proving `f|_{𝔾_a}` is an additive homomorphism requires killing the
additive-defect map `ψ(x,y)=f(x+y)−f(x)−f(y)`, which lives on the affine surface `𝔾_a×𝔾_a`; to
apply the (complete-source) Rigidity Lemma one must FIRST extend ψ to the complete `ℙ¹×ℙ¹` (the
addition does not extend), which IS Thm 3.2's surface case = Milne Thm 3.4 → Lemma 3.3. So the
surface rational-map-extension gap (no Mathlib Weil divisors) is genuinely on the genus-0 critical
path and is the route's riskiest piece. I hand-corrected `rmk:thm32_codim1_mathlib_gap` and the
`lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial` proof to state this correctly before the review.

## Decision made

**(A) Commit Route C (Milne §I.3), excise the cube.** Why: the cube is on NEITHER the genus-0 base
case NOR Route A's Albanese UP (source-verified twice); building it now is a pure ~8–15-iter
zero-Mathlib tax. Route C reuses the just-proven Rigidity Lemma maximally, is uniformly char-free
(no Frobenius epicycle), and is the least Mathlib-blocked path (valuative criterion present).
LOC/risk: ~15–30 iters; the single substantive risk is the surface divisor gap (Lemma 3.3), honestly
flagged OPEN (pointwise valuative side-step may avoid building Weil-divisor theory). Cheapest
reversal signal: if the surface extension proves intractable in Mathlib over several iters, reweigh
(c-hybrid)/(b) — but the analogist shows those are worse.

**(B) Fire ONE deep prover lane this iter on AVR.lean: Cor 1.5 (`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity`)
then Cor 1.2 (`av_regularMap_isHom_of_zero`).** No-regret (both feed Route A's Albanese UP too),
directly from the proven `rigidity_lemma`, and the gate is freshly cleared (fast-path). Cor 1.5 is
the foundational additivity; Cor 1.2 follows. Deep lane → PARTIAL acceptable.

## Subagent skips

- progress-critic: the only active route (Rigidity-Lemma chain) COMPLETED in the prior iter (iter-162,
  sorry→0 for the chain); the genus-0 base-case route is freshly decided THIS iter with zero prover
  trajectory to extrapolate from. Per its skip condition ("the only active route just completed in
  the prior iter"), there is no convergence signal to assess. Will re-dispatch once the Cor 1.5 lane
  produces ≥1 iter of trajectory.

## For iter-164

1. Collect the Cor 1.5 / Cor 1.2 lane. If both close axiom-clean → the §I.3 additivity foundation is
   in place; next targets are Thm 3.2 (the surface rational-map extension — the riskiest sub-build;
   consider a mathlib-analogist cross-domain consult on "extend a rational map to a proper/group
   target across codim-1 on a smooth surface, no Weil divisors") and the `𝔾_a/𝔾_m` triviality.
2. The genus-0⟹ℙ¹ RR bridge (`genusZero_curve_iso_P1`) remains a parallel sub-build (no Mathlib RR).
3. Re-dispatch progress-critic (trajectory now exists).

     `[GeometricallyIrreducible (V⊗W).hom]`, `[LocallyOfFiniteType (V⊗W).hom]`, `[IsReduced (V⊗W).left]`)
     and `A` an abelian variety (`[GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]`,
     mirroring `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const`'s bundle), a morphism `h : V ⊗ W ⟶ A` based at the
     identity decomposes as `h = (f ≫ p) · (g ≫ q)` (the GrpObj hom-set product; **not** assuming
     commutativity — see the blueprint "Lean encoding" paragraph). PROOF: form the GrpObj difference
     `φ := h · (f∘p · g∘q)⁻¹`, show it collapses the `V`-axis (matching `rigidity_lemma`'s `_hf` hyp
     shape `lift (𝟙 V) (toUnit V ≫ w₀) ≫ φ = toUnit V ≫ η[A]`), apply `rigidity_lemma` to get
     `φ = snd V W ≫ g'`, then use that `φ` also vanishes on the `{v₀}×W` section of `snd` to force
     `g' = const η[A]`, hence `φ ≡ η[A]`. The `+`/`·` on `Hom(V⊗W, A)` is the `GrpObj`-induced
